provenance.notes: Processed 9 quotes from 10 verse files. Source: HistoricalChristianFaith/Commentaries-Database. License: PUBLIC DOMAIN.

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: We receive of the Holy Spirit in order that we may be made spiritual; for the natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God.

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God.

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: And if any one do so, then, after the sharp vengeance which is due to such a crime, and which is justly to be carried out against the sacrilegious, let him be condemned to perpetual infamy, and east into prison or consigned to life-long exile. For, according to the apostle,

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: Behold, to what does the wisdom of this world urge a man? To live in pleasures. Whence it is said: A widow that liveth in pleasure, is dead while she liveth.

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: For to what, most dearly beloved, does the wisdom of this world urge us, but to seek things that are hurtful, and to love things that are to perish, and to neglect things that are healthful, and to esteem as of no value things that are lasting? It commends the love of money, of which it is said, The love of money is the root of all evil;

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: And Joseph, who by the apostles was surnamed Barnabas (which is, being interpreted, the son of consolation), a Levite, and of the country of Cyprus, having land, sold it, and brought the money, and laid it at the apostles' feet; "

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: And the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband. And great fear came upon all the Church, and upon as many as heard these things."

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: ) rather things above, and not things on the earth;

data.author: Pope Urban I
data.quote: Whose soever sins ye remit, are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained."
